content: Comparative analyses of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM vs RND vs hand-designed curious agents) can be reframed as an active learning problem where the agent itself adaptively selects which intrinsic reward formulation to use based on what it has not yet explored about its own exploratory capabilities, stored in its episodic memory. This creates a meta-curious agent that improves its own curiosity mechanism over time as it explores new environments.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
